scope.description: A downstream claim boundary created after explicit CCE expansion.

positive_claims.statement: The artifact is eligible for limited internal review routing under the stated internal review process.
positive_claims.limited_to: Limited internal review routing only; not deployment approval, legal sufficiency, compliance sufficiency, general safety, or source truth.

non_claims.statement: This boundary asserts limited internal-review eligibility only and does not assert deployment approval.
The benchmark execution record non-claims remain preserved and are not converted into deployment, safety, legal, or production claims.
Human or institutional review remains required before legal, compliance, safety, or deployment conclusions are drawn.
This boundary does not assert that all relevant upstream sources or context were captured.
Preserved upstream non-claim remains represented in this downstream CBC so the inherited boundary remains inspectable without relying on an aggregate summary alone.

upstream_claims_received.claim_text: The referenced benchmark execution record structurally records the recorded metric result within the stated verification scope.

upstream_claims_relied_on.claim_text: The referenced benchmark execution record structurally records the recorded metric result within the stated verification scope.

transitivity_policy.statement: This claim boundary does not transfer into broader safety, compliance, legal, truth, deployment, or correctness claims without an explicit downstream claim boundary.

issuer_semantics_authority.statement: Issuer-defined terms are bounded by this record and do not create external certification authority.

evidence_refs.description: Placeholder downstream routing record for v0.2.2 boundary example.

known_gaps.description: Production equivalence has not been established by the benchmark execution record.
known_gaps.effect: The downstream claim is limited to review routing and does not support deployment readiness.

human_or_institutional_decisions_remaini.description: Any legal, compliance, deployment, or policy interpretation remains outside this structural boundary.
